title: Assemblers (vehicles and metal goods)
description: Directly supervise and coordinate the activities of production and operating workers, such as inspectors, precision workers, machine setters and operators, assemblers, fabricators, and plant and system operators. Excludes team or work leaders. Assemble, fit, fasten, and install parts of airplanes, space vehicles, or missiles, such as tails, wings, fuselage, bulkheads, stabilizers, landing gear, rigging and control equipment, or heating and ventilating systems. Wind wire coils used in electrical components, such as resistors and transformers, and in electrical equipment and instruments, such as field cores, bobbins, armature cores, electrical motors, generators, and control equipment. (Based on 26 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Fabricate parts needed for assembly or installation using shop machinery or equipment, and assemble parts, fittings, or subassemblies on aircraft using layout tools, hand tools, power tools, or fasteners.
Read blueprints, illustrations, or specifications to determine layouts, sequences of operations, or identities or relationships of parts, and plan assembly operations accordingly.
Cut, trim, file, bend, or smooth parts to ensure proper fit and clearance, and lay out, drill, ream, tap, or cut parts for assembly as needed.
Adjust, repair, rework, or replace parts or assemblies to ensure proper operation, and inspect or test installed components for fit, alignment, performance, or compliance with standards.
Fasten or install piping, fixtures, or wiring and electrical components to form assemblies or subassemblies using hand tools, rivet guns, or welding equipment.

Read and interpret assembly blueprints or specifications manuals and communicate assembly procedures or techniques to other workers, as well as supervise assemblers and train employees on job procedures.
Position or align components for assembly manually or using hoists, and connect cables, tubes, and wiring according to specifications.
Inspect, operate, and test completed products to verify functioning and conformance to customer specifications while being responsible for maintaining production equipment and machinery.
Maintain, clean, and lubricate parts or components and work areas to ensure efficient production processes.
Record production data such as weights, types, quantities, and storage locations of materials and complete production reports to communicate team production levels to management.
Load, unload, or adjust materials or products on conveyors by hand or using lifts and hoists, and distribute materials to work stations as needed.
Perform test production runs and make adjustments as necessary to ensure that completed products meet standards and specifications.

Remove jammed materials from machines and readjust components as necessary to resume normal operations. Correct machinery malfunctions by performing actions such as removing jams, and inform supervisors of malfunctions as necessary.
Align and position materials being joined to ensure accurate application of adhesive or heat sealing, and fill machines with glue, cement, or adhesives.

Observe gauges, meters, and control panels to obtain information about equipment temperatures and pressures, or the speed of feeders or conveyors, and monitor machine operations to detect malfunctions and report or resolve problems.
Remove and stack completed materials or products, and restock materials to be joined.

Clean and maintain gluing and cementing machines, using solutions, lubricants, brushes, and scrapers. Scrape, dislodge, or break excess frost, ice, or frozen product from equipment to prevent accumulation, using hands and hand tools.
Read work orders and communicate with coworkers to determine machine and equipment settings and adjustments and supply and product specifications.

Adjust machine components according to specifications such as widths, lengths, and thickness of materials and amounts of glue, cement, or adhesive required. Monitor pressure gauges, ammeters, flowmeters, thermometers, or products, and adjust controls to maintain specified conditions, such as feed rate, product consistency, temperature, air pressure, and machine speed.

Examine and measure completed materials or products to verify conformance to specifications, using measuring devices such as tape measures, gauges, or calipers. Inspect workpieces for defects, and measure workpieces to determine accuracy of machine operation, using rules, templates, or other measuring instruments.

Mount or load material such as paper, plastic, wood, or rubber in feeding mechanisms of cementing or gluing machines, and transport materials, supplies, and finished products between storage and work areas, using forklifts.

Start machinery, such as pumps, feeders, or conveyors, and turn valves to heat, admit, or transfer products, refrigerants, or mixes. Turn valves or pull levers to start and regulate the flow of coolant or lubricant to work areas.

Select, install, and adjust alignment of drills, cutters, dies, guides, and holding devices, using templates, measuring instruments, and hand tools. Set machine stops or guides to specified lengths as indicated by scales, rules, or templates.

Read blueprints or job orders to determine product specifications and tooling instructions and to plan operational sequences. Study blueprints, layouts, sketches, or work orders to assess workpiece specifications and to determine tooling instructions, tools and materials needed, and sequences of operations.

Fit and assemble parts to make or repair machine tools. Assemble equipment, and attach pipes, fittings, or valves, using hand tools. Select machine tooling to be used, using knowledge of machine and production requirements.

Inspect etched work for depth of etching, uniformity, and defects, using calibrated microscopes, gauges, fingers, or magnifying lenses. Measure, examine, or test completed units to check for defects and ensure conformance to specifications, using precision instruments, such as micrometers.
Perform minor machine maintenance, such as oiling or cleaning machines, dies, or workpieces, or adding coolant to machine reservoirs, and maintain machine tools in proper operational condition.

Remove workpieces from machines, and check to ensure that they conform to specifications, using measuring instruments such as microscopes, gauges, calipers, and micrometers. Record temperatures, amounts of materials processed, or test results on report forms.
Inspect and flush lines with solutions or steam, and spray equipment with sterilizing solutions.
Clean work area. Dispose of scrap or waste material in accordance with company policies and environmental regulations.
Operate equipment to verify operational efficiency. Record operational data, such as pressure readings, lengths of strokes, feed rates, or speeds.

Observe machine operations to detect any problems, making necessary adjustments to correct problems, and adjust machine or freezer speed and air intake to obtain desired consistency and amount of product.

Lift and position workpieces, manually or with hoists, and secure them in hoppers or on machine tables, faceplates, or chucks, using clamps. Position and secure workpieces on machines, using holding devices, measuring instruments, hand tools, and hoists.
Mount and position tools in machine chucks, spindles, or other tool holding devices, using hand tools, and install tools in spindles.

Activate machine start-up switches to grind, lap, hone, debar, shear, or cut workpieces, according to specifications, and start machines to produce sample workpieces, monitoring their operations to detect malfunctions.

Move machine controls to index workpieces, adjust machines for pre-selected operational settings, set distances between rolls, and turn handles or knobs to set pressures and depths of ram strokes to synchronize machine operations.
Brush or spray lubricating compounds on workpieces, turn valves to direct coolant flow over cutting areas, and ensure that workpieces are properly lubricated and cooled during machine operation.
Repair or replace machine parts using hand tools and notify engineering personnel when corrective action is required; troubleshoot, maintain, and make minor repairs to equipment.
Measure and inspect machined parts to ensure conformance to product specifications, and verify that workpieces conform to specifications using measuring instruments.
Read work orders or blueprints to determine specified tolerances and sequences of operations for machine setup and to determine specifications for materials, cutting lines, or dimensions.
Remove dies from machines when production runs are finished and change dies on extruding machines according to production line changes.

Set up, operate, or tend machines to saw, cut, shear, slit, punch, crimp, notch, bend, or straighten metal or plastic material, and start operation of rolling and milling machines to flatten or reduce material.
Examine completed workpieces for defects, such as chipped edges or marred surfaces, and sort defective pieces according to types of flaws.
Position, align, and secure workpieces against fixtures or stops on machine beds or dies using hand tools and measuring devices; secure and direct workpieces using guides, stops, and holding blocks.
Measure completed workpieces to verify conformance to specifications with precision instruments, and inspect materials and finished products for defects.
Perform minor assembly, such as fastening parts with nuts, bolts, or screws, using power tools or hand tools.
Lift workpieces onto work tables either manually or with hoists, and direct crane operators to lift and position workpieces, while ensuring proper handling of materials.
Direct and coordinate the activities of employees engaged in the production or processing of goods, and confer with management to resolve worker problems, complaints, or grievances.
Enforce safety and sanitation regulations, and monitor work areas to ensure compliance with production standards and safety measures.
Maintain operations data such as time, production, and cost records, and prepare management reports of production results, while keeping records of employees' attendance and hours worked.
Evaluate employee performance and recommend personnel actions, such as hirings, evaluations, or promotions based on performance and operational needs.
Plan and establish work schedules, assignments, and production sequences to meet production goals.
Calculate labor and equipment requirements and production specifications using standard formulas, and determine budgets, production goals, and rates based on company policies.
Monitor machine cycles and mill operation to detect any malfunctions and adjust controls for efficient operation.
Conduct employee training in equipment operations or work and safety procedures, or assign employee training to experienced workers. Set up and adjust machines and equipment.
